100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 11, 1926, the United States celebrated the successful launch of the first transcontinental air mail service, connecting New York and San Francisco. Meanwhile, in Europe, tensions rose as the League of Nations convened to address the ongoing disputes in the Ruhr Valley. In the realm of sports, the 1926 World Series of Baseball garnered attention as teams prepared for the summer season.
